name: Oz
description: Oz platform for Linux server administration tasks: running shell commands, checking server health metrics, fetching application or Docker logs, deploying projects, launching AI agents for infrastructure automation. Also handles server diagnostics, troubleshooting, debugging, and analysis of why servers or applications are failing or slow.
action_notes: For greetings, social chat, or non-infrastructure questions → call greet
For quick bash/shell commands → call run_bash
For application or system log inspection → call app_logs
For Docker containers on THIS machine or an SSH-connected server (not a named remote Docker endpoint) → call docker_logs

For Proxmox VE operations (VMs, LXC containers, nodes, storage, cluster) AND creating new VMs → call proxmox. VM names (rohit-vm, web-server, db-01) are NOT server/endpoint names — do not pass them as the server argument
For Docker operations on a remote Docker API endpoint registered in Oz (e.g. 'linode', 'vps', 'prod-docker') → call docker_logs — it auto-detects Docker endpoints and routes correctly
For deploying a project (git pull, install, restart) → call deploy
For listing registered servers → call list_servers
For checking if a website is up/down → call check_website

When a SERVER/HOST name is mentioned for SSH or Docker operations, pass it as the server argument. But for Proxmox: only pass server if the user names a Proxmox cluster/endpoint — never set server to a VM name
For operational infrastructure tasks (deploy, configure, install, setup, backup, restore, migrate, monitor) → call n8n_agent
For investigative/analytical tasks (diagnose, troubleshoot, investigate, debug, analyze, find root cause) → call launch_agent
If unsure which action fits, evaluate: is the user asking to DO something (operational) or to FIGURE OUT something (investigative)? Operational → n8n_agent. Investigative → launch_agent.
If the intent is still unclear, call n8n_agent with confirmed=false to ask the user

greet.description: Handle greetings, social questions, and anything not related to servers or infrastructure. Use for: hello, hi, how are you, what can you do, who are you, thanks, goodbye.

run_bash.description: Run a bash or shell command on this machine or a named server. Use for quick one-shot commands: docker ps, df -h, free -m, ls, curl, etc.

server.description: Target server name to run the command on. Empty means local machine.

utterance_samples: run uptime on the staging server

server_health.description: Check the health of a server: CPU, memory, disk, load average, and failed services. For Docker-specific status use docker_logs.

server.description: Target server name to check health on. Empty means local machine.

utterance_samples: check health of the database server
show CPU and memory usage on prod
disk space check on production server
check failed services on the server
run health check on web server
what is the CPU and memory usage on the production server
show me disk usage and load average on staging

app_logs.description: Retrieve and inspect application or system logs from a server. Supports log file paths, service names, and keyword filtering.

server.description: Target server name to fetch logs from. Empty means local machine.

path.description: Log file path or service name to inspect.

lines.description: Number of log lines to fetch.

utterance_samples: fetch nginx error logs on prod-web
show logs for the api service on staging
fetch logs on production filtered by ERROR
tail the app logs on the web server
show recent errors in the application logs
get last 50 lines from /var/log/syslog on db-server

docker_logs.description: Get Docker container logs, status, count, and health. Use for Docker operations on THIS machine, an SSH-connected server, or a named remote Docker endpoint registered in Oz (like 'linode', 'vps', 'prod-docker'). Automatically detects whether the named server is a Docker API endpoint and routes accordingly. For general server health (CPU/memory/disk) use server_health.

server.description: Target server name for Docker operations. Empty means local machine.

lines.description: Number of log lines to fetch.

utterance_samples: show me docker logs for nginx
check the api container logs on prod-web
get last 100 lines of logs for the postgres container
why is the redis container crashing
show docker container status on staging
check all docker containers on the server
get logs from the web container with filter ERROR
is the nginx container running on production
how many docker containers are running on server AIT5252
how many containers are running on prod-web
show running containers on the server
how many docker containers are running on linode
how many containers are running on my docker endpoint

proxmox.description: Manage and query a Proxmox VE hypervisor cluster. Use for: listing VMs and containers, checking VM/node status, starting/stopping/rebooting/suspending VMs or LXC containers, checking storage, viewing cluster health, node resource usage, AND creating new VMs from cloud-init templates (e.g. 'create a new Ubuntu VM named web-server'). Works on any registered Proxmox endpoint. IMPORTANT: VM names (like 'rohit-vm', 'web-server', 'ubuntu-01') are NOT Proxmox endpoint names — leave the server parameter empty unless the user explicitly names a Proxmox endpoint.

server.description: Proxmox endpoint/cluster name only if user explicitly names one (e.g. 'on my home-proxmox'). Do NOT set this to a VM name or hostname. Leave empty to use the default registered endpoint.

utterance_samples: how many VMs are running on proxmox
reboot the proxmox VM named web-server
what is the proxmox node CPU usage
create a new Ubuntu VM named rohit-vm
create a new VM called web-server with Ubuntu
provision a new Ubuntu server named db-01
create Ubuntu VM named test-server with 40GB disk
spin up a new VM with Ubuntu 24.04 called api-server
create a new virtual machine named staging-01
make a new Ubuntu VM called dev-box
provision a VM named prod-web with static IP 192.168.1.50
show network interfaces on pve node

deploy.description: Use ONLY for git-based project deployment: git pull, install dependencies, build, and restart a service. NOT for deploying containers or Docker images — use n8n_agent for that.

server.description: Target server name to deploy to. Empty means local machine.

path.description: Project directory path on the server.

service.description: Service name to restart after deploy.

utterance_samples: deploy the project at /var/www/app on prod-web
deploy to production from the main branch
pull and restart the api service on staging
update and restart the nginx service on prod
deploy the app from branch develop on staging-server

n8n_agent.description: Use for operational infrastructure tasks: deployments, configuration, installation, setup, backups, restores, migrations, monitoring, and any other concrete action on servers or containers. Routes the user's request to an external workflow that can execute it. First ask with confirmed=false, then call with confirmed=true if user agrees. For investigative/analytical tasks (diagnose, troubleshoot, debug) use launch_agent instead.

agent_name.description: The n8n agent name that best matches the user's request (e.g. devops, k8s, backup). Leave empty if unsure.

task.description: The task or question to send to the n8n agent.

confirmed.description: Set to false to ask the user first. Set to true only if the user already agreed to use n8n.

utterance_samples: deploy nginx docker container with custom port 8989
deploy nginx on port 8989 and give me the container ID
set up a new web server
install nginx on the production server
configure monitoring for the api service
back up the database to S3
migrate the application to the new server
ask the devops n8n agent to deploy nginx

launch_agent.description: Use for investigative/analytical infrastructure tasks: diagnose why something is failing, troubleshoot errors, debug issues, analyze logs or metrics, find root causes, investigate anomalies. For concrete operational tasks (deploy, configure, install, setup, backup, monitor) use n8n_agent instead.

prompt.description: The full user request or task description to give to the agent.

utterance_samples: investigate why the API is returning 500 errors on prod
analyse the logs and find what is causing slowdowns
troubleshoot the failing deployment on staging
debug why the service keeps restarting on prod-web
diagnose the database connection issue on the server
check why the network is slow on the database server
find out why the backup is failing
investigate the high memory usage on the web server
analyse the error logs and tell me what is wrong
why is the nginx container crashing on production
I need help with something on the server
can you handle a server task for me
help me with an infrastructure issue

check_website.description: Check if a website is up/down from server-side. Reports HTTP status code, response time, and any errors. Can optionally check from a specific server (external perspective).

url.description: The website URL to check (e.g. https://example.com).

domain.description: Alternative to url: the domain name to check (e.g. example.com).

server.description: Optional server to check FROM (external perspective). Empty means check from this machine.

utterance_samples: check https://staging.example.com from the production server
can you check if server with IP 192.168.1.1 is online
check if remote server 10.0.0.1 is reachable
ping server 192.168.1.100 to see if it responds

list_agents.description: List recent agent runs and their current status (completed, running, failed).
utterance_samples: show me the last agent tasks

get_agent.description: Get details, status and full output logs of a specific agent run by its ID.

cancel_agent.description: Cancel a currently running agent by its ID.

list_servers.description: List all registered servers in Oz, showing their names, hosts, and authentication type.
utterance_samples: which servers are configured in Oz
